reasoning: ma' appears to negate a state; ek then denotes the sleep state, so ma' ek = awake.
The pi'an element marks belongings/possessions; with ma' and puk this yields the notion relating to trust with belongings.
puuy denotes strength/firmness; its negation gives tired/weak.
thayan relates to fear; ma' thayan thus gives brave.
mee' is the base word for eye.
Adding thayan to a noun forms a related object; here it yields crab.
mee' combined with weep yields fingernail.
min corresponds to heart.
ngak on its own corresponds to good.
ngak before mee' gives a device for the hands—handcuffs.
ngak min ‘good heart’ expresses happy.
ngak used with way gives a positive water-related state—fresh water.
ngangk stands for law.
ngangk ek marks a negative/undesirable water state—undrinkable water.
ngangk min contrasts with ngak min; it yields sad.
Adding thayan to ngangk yields the shoulder blade.
ngangk way intensifies the sleep state—sound asleep.
puuy is strong/firm.
puuy ek specifies the big/primary finger—thumb.
thayan on its own denotes water.
weep thayan gives the shell of the crab.
wik kiith refers to the English language.
wik thayan denotes a water source—spring.
The base used in the compound for handcuffs; it serves as the word for hand.
Bad is expressed as ‘not good’: ma' + puk.
weep is the element that forms fingernail in mee' weep.
ma' puk literally ‘not good’ = bad.
